tweets.text:
Earlier this week I called DeepSWE the best coding benchmark out there. I meant it. It's the one that matches what these models actually feel like day to day.

Then Opus 4.8 shipped without a number on the board, so I went to add one myself. That's where I learned how little of a https://t.co/w49depdWP1

tweets.text:
This chart is going around as "FT data" proving AI is a money pit: Microsoft -9%, Meta -29%, Oracle -36%.

It's not FT data. I checked the chart. The numbers come from Panmure Liberum, a brokerage. The FT just ran it.

Then the subtitle: "implied return, 2025-30, assuming zero https://t.co/w79SWisLW9
tweets.name: Paweł Huryn

tweets.text:
4.6 guessed what you meant.
4.7 stopped guessing.
4.8 stops pretending it's done.

Two upgrades on the same axis: honesty. https://t.co/mQVQg3tO3t

tweets.text:
Tested Claude Code's new dynamic workflows. 8 agents in 24.5s. 253k tokens.

The launch post frames it as a large fleet of coordinated subagents. That part isn't new. The SDK has had parallel calls for years.

The new part is where the orchestrator runs. It's JavaScript, not a https://t.co/PxLpU8wwPS

tweets.text:
Capabilities and bug fixes shipped to Grok Build for VS Code v 1.2.0 (ACP wrapper)

• Plan mode implemented (despite CLI limits)
• Three-verdict plan review with a comment
• Plan markdown rendering
• Per-session plan history
• Native-Windows webview fixes
• Testing — 178 https://t.co/7OPg7qgR2h https://t.co/3iYRmFKihW

tweets.text:
I built a Claude Code command that audits the code you already shipped, not just the diff you're about to commit.

Run it on your repo, or scope it to one area. The point isn't volume. Most scanners bury you because reporting everything is free. This one tries to kill each https://t.co/jGmAwAjdHN
tweets.name: Paweł Huryn

tweets.text:
Grok Build v1.1.0 for VS Code has shipped. New:
- Windows support
- Approve by default
- Inline diffs

Open source + marketplace: https://t.co/QOaVmc4i6o https://t.co/f4ZpGvHlxS
tweets.name: Paweł Huryn

tweets.text:
Claude Code's new security plugin has one missing mode: static audit.

It guards the code Claude writes next:
- edits
- turn diffs
- commits
- pushes

It does not audit the repo you already have.

So I made the missing command:

`/security-audit-static`

It adapts the public https://t.co/zSs5iPOgUF https://t.co/h1iUJrV2Av
tweets.name: Paweł Huryn
